commands.review_summary:
The PR hoists the Feishu abortable-delay timer binding so the cleanup closure can read it before timeout assignment completes.

PR surface: Source +1. Total +1 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: current main can call `finish(false)` before the `const timer` binding is initialized, and `finish()` reads that binding during cleanup. I did not establish a live real-setup reproduction artifact.

target.title: fix: hoist timer declaration to avoid TDZ ReferenceError in abortable delay
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes a latent TDZ bug where `finish()` references `timer` declared as `const` after `finish` was defined, risking `ReferenceError` if abort fires before timer assignment.

## Why This Change Was Made

Hoist `timer` as `let` above `finish` so it is safely `undefined` when `finish` fires early.

## User Impact

No user-visible change. Removes fragile declaration-order dependency.

## Evidence

```
extensions/feishu/src/async.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  5 passed (5)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 9.75s
```
